data.title: Stock market futures up, gold at all-time high despite looming shutdown. Dow +0.27%, Nasdaq +0.44%, S&P 500 +0.34%. Gold $3,815.28.Risk of economic data blackout.
data.description: Stock market futures in the U.S. rose on Monday morning despite an 83% probability of a government shutdown, with Dow Jones futures up 127 points (0.27%), Nasdaq 100 futures gaining 108 points (0.44%), and S&P 500 futures rising by 0.34%. Gold also reached a new all-time high at $3,815.28 per ounce. The calm market reaction is attributed to the limited immediate financial impact of a potential shutdown compared to the risks of a prolonged political impasse. However, concerns remain about a blackout of crucial economic data that could leave investors and policymakers without essential information. Jim Bianco, President at Bianco Research LLC, stated that the market's initial response is rational given historical patterns of minimal shock.

data.title: US households facing increasing financial strain as grocery prices surge by 29%, dollar loses 25% value, affordability worsens.
data.description: American households are experiencing increased financial pressure due to rising costs of essential items, surpassing wage growth and diminishing purchasing ability. An Affordability Crisis is exacerbating as noted in The Kobeissi Letter, with basic necessities becoming more unaffordable. Since February 2020, core expenses have surged significantly, with grocery prices hitting an all-time high with a 29% increase, and overall food prices rising by 30%. Arbor Data Science data reveals that the cost burden extends beyond groceries, with food away from home prices surging by 33% and transportation services increasing by 36%. This leaves families with constrained budgets. The U.S. Dollar's purchasing power is also decreasing rapidly. Fed Chair's warning of "No Risk-Free Path" has led to an unusual outcome, with both Gold and Stocks hitting new records simultaneously. More details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Peter Schiff warns that Eric Trump's Fed prediction hints at White House's understanding of economy's weakness, potential for soaring inflation.
data.description: Economist Peter Schiff is expressing concerns about a recent statement made by Eric Trump, son of President Donald Trump, suggesting potential alignment with White House views on the U.S. economy. Schiff commented on Trump's prediction of the Federal Reserve reverting to quantitative easing, a policy involving bond purchases to boost liquidity. The implication is that if Eric believes this, the President may share the view on monetary policy. Schiff highlights that this forecast may reveal insights into the President's intentions for the Federal Reserve amidst an impending power transition. The full story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Rendina and RWJBarnabas Health open 71,000 sq ft ambulatory care center in Old Bridge, NJ on Route 9.
data.description: In Old Bridge, New Jersey, on October 3, 2025, Rendina Healthcare Real Estate and RWJBarnabas Health marked the unveiling of RWJBarnabas Health at Old Bridge, a cutting-edge ambulatory care center spanning 71,000 square feet located at 1980 Route 9. A ribbon-cutting event was conducted to commemorate the occasion.

data.title: Federal court denies nChain UK Ltd.'s motion to dismiss in music industry blockchain breach case, allowing lawsuit to proceed.
data.description: On October 3, 2025, in Atlanta, there was a notable development in the music and blockchain sectors as the U.S. District Court for the Northern District of Georgia rejected nChain UK Ltd.'s request for dismissal in a breach of contract lawsuit. This decision marks a win for innovation in these industries and signifies progress in legal proceedings related to the case. The specifics of the case, including important figures and further details, were not provided in the rephrased text.

data.title: Firefly Rocket test explodes causing 20% stock drop; AST SpaceMobile BlueBird 6 ready for flight. Space sector update.
data.description: Space stocks are experiencing movement due to various news events, including a significant explosion, the finalization of new contracts, and the anticipation of upcoming launches. One notable development is the surge in ASTS stock. On the other hand, Firefly Aerospace, Inc. (NASDAQ: FLY) witnessed a considerable drop of over 20% following a testing mishap that resulted in the destruction of the core stage of its Alpha rocket meant for the next mission. Fortunately, Firefly confirmed the safety of all personnel and assured that no other facilities were affected by the incident. The explosion was captured by a CCTV camera during a test flight in Texas, and a video of the event was shared by USA Today on social media. Firefly emphasized the importance of regular testing as part of its operational philosophy. For further details, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Walmart is removing artificial colors and 30 additives from US private-label food brands in response to consumer feedback.
data.description: Walmart Inc. (NYSE: WMT) is set to eliminate synthetic dyes and around 30 other additives from its U.S. private-label food lines. This extensive overhaul includes removing certain preservatives, artificial sweeteners, and fat substitutes from popular brands like Great Value, Marketside, Freshness Guaranteed, and bettergoods. The decision, spearheaded by Walmart U.S. President and CEO John Furner, is based on direct consumer feedback requesting products with simpler, more recognizable ingredients. Furner stated, "Our customers want products made with familiar ingredients, and we've responded to their input." This initiative marks one of the most significant changes made by a major retailer in recent times and highlights Walmart's commitment to meeting consumer demands.
WH Press Sec. Leavitt: Trump has several meetings on the books today.

data.title: Bezos warns of AI investment bubble, foresees it transforming all industries despite risks.
data.description: A prominent billionaire entrepreneur has expressed concerns over the current surge in funding for artificial intelligence (AI) projects, highlighting potential warning signs. However, the successful business magnate also holds strong belief in the transformative power of AI technology, stating that it will revolutionize various industries. The entrepreneur's statements reflect the growing importance and impact of AI in modern society, with investments in the sector reaching unprecedented levels.

data.title: Kronos Worldwide, Inc. seeks to raise €75 million through private offering of 9.50% Senior Secured Notes due 2029.
data.description: Kronos Worldwide, Inc. is looking to raise debt capital through a private offering of €75 million aggregate principal amount of additional 9.50% Senior Secured Notes due 2029 by its subsidiary Kronos International, Inc. This offering will add to the existing €351,174,000 aggregate principal amount of 9.50% Senior Secured Notes due 2029 issued by KII. The new Notes have the same terms as the Existing Notes. The Notes will trade separately under different ISIN and Common Code numbers for 40 days post the issue date before being maintained under the same numbers as the Existing Notes.

data.title: WallachBeth Capital to induce Healthcare Triangle Warrant, raising $755,000. Leader in digital solutions.
data.description: On October 3, 2025, in Jersey City, New Jersey, WallachBeth Capital LLC revealed that Healthcare Triangle, Inc. (Nasdaq: HCTI), a prominent company in digital transformation solutions, is partnering with them. WallachBeth is known for its capital markets and institutional execution services. This collaboration signifies a significant step for HCTI, showcasing their continued growth and development in the industry.

data.title: Global FSO communication market to grow from USD 2.04B in 2025 to USD 41.85B by 2035, at CAGR of 31.63%. Dominance by point-to-point FSO.
data.description: In Dublin, a report on the Free Space Optics (FSO) Communication Market has been released, now available on ResearchAndMarkets.com. The report covers industry trends and forecasts up to 2035, detailing factors such as the type of platform, component, transmission range, FSO communication, areas of application, end-users, company size, and key geographical regions. The global market for free space optics communication is projected to increase from USD 2.04 billion in 2025 to USD 41.85 billion by 2035, indicating a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 31.63%.
